official_functions.role: Name the packet id assigned in Play clientbound state

official_functions.function_or_member: ClientboundContainerSetContentPacket(int, int, List<ItemStack>, ItemStack), ClientboundContainerSetContentPacket.STREAM_CODEC, ByteBufCodecs.CONTAINER_ID, ByteBufCodecs.VAR_INT, ItemStack.OPTIONAL_LIST_STREAM_CODEC, ItemStack.OPTIONAL_STREAM_CODEC
official_functions.role: Construct, write, decode, and observe the container id, state id, item list, and carried item body
Write and dispatch-decode the full official Play clientbound container_set_content frame

invariants: The official Play clientbound table assigns container_set_content immediately after container_close.
The official Play clientbound protocol codec dispatches the container_set_content id to ClientboundContainerSetContentPacket.
The official container_set_content body is containerId, stateId, item list, and carried item in STREAM_CODEC order.

The fixture uses the official ClientboundContainerSetContentPacket constructor with an empty item list and ItemStack.EMPTY carried item, so it does not require an initialized Menu, screen, Level, inventory, item registry entry, component registry payload, or game state.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, decoded container id, decoded state id, decoded item count, decoded carried-item emptiness, Play clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte count are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.

This contract does not prove non-empty ItemStack/component registry handling, menu lifecycle behavior, inventory state, initialized game state, runtime Play entry, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer non-empty ItemStack/component registry handling, menu lifecycle behavior, inventory state, initialized game state, runtime Play entry, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
